data.title: Strategy Stock Has Price Target Slashed – But Is Still a Buy, Says Clear Street
data.description:
Bitcoin seems to be staging a recovery at the moment, up by 7% over the past day, so it's no surprise to find Strategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) shares are also on the move, up by 11% at the time of writing.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The gain in Wednesday's session should boost sentiment among investors, as the stock has endured a brutal pullback, shedding 59% over the past 6 months.

The depressed price action mirrors the general downbeat trajectory Bitcoin has taken and has led Clear Street analyst Brian Dobson to revise his outlook. Reflecting "persistently lower BTC prices and more conservative BTC yield assumptions," Dobson has lowered his target from $268 to $198, although the revised figure still makes room for 12-month appreciation of 34%. Dobson's rating stays a Buy. (To watch Dobson's track record, click here)

While Dobson is less optimistic on the near-term action, he keeps an overall bullish stance following a recent meeting with Strategy's executive chairman and co-founder Michael Saylor and president and CEO Phong Le. "We continue to view MicroStrategy as a highly differentiated and scalable vehicle for Bitcoin exposure," said Dobson. "The company's treasury strategy has proven durable, with BTC holdings now exceeding 673k BTC. While volatility remains elevated, we believe disciplined balance sheet management, a growing NAV base, and consistent growth in Bitcoin per share remain core drivers of long-term value creation."

As for key takeaways from the meeting, Dobson says the company plans to lean into preferred share issuance in the first half of 2026, using "structured capital" to fund additional BTC purchases while preserving "equity optionality" and strengthening STRC's "institutional positioning." STRC refers to Strategy's perpetual preferred stock that pays variable dividends, has no maturity, and was created to raise capital efficiently. Management emphasized that STRC is the company's strategic priority and a cornerstone of its next phase in capital markets development. The goal isn't just to hold BTC, but to build an institutional-grade instrument that offers deep, liquid trading, lower volatility than spot BTC, and operates as a fully regulated crypto credit vehicle.

On the regulatory front, management sees the next major milestone coming after passage of the Clarity Act: a potential US withdrawal from the Basel framework, which currently imposes steep capital charges on BTC holdings, effectively discouraging meaningful balance sheet exposure. "While likely more than a year away," Dobson said, "reform could materially improve BTC adoption among legacy financial institutions."

The company's thesis is that BTC is evolving from a speculative asset into a "sovereign-adjacent reserve asset." As institutional and sovereign capital shifts from gold to BTC, its scale grows, volatility drops, and it becomes "balance sheet infrastructure." At that stage, a BTC credit sector can emerge, and MSTR aims to be the "benchmark BTC credit issuer."

"Strategically," Dobson added, "MSTR is evolving from BTC proxy to BTC treasury to BTC-backed credit platform, with STRC serving as the bridge toward sovereign-grade crypto capital market infrastructure."

Turning now to the rest of the Street, where there have been 9 other recent MSTR reviews, all also positive, making the consensus view a Strong Buy. At $310, the average target points toward one-year returns of 111%. (See MSTR stock forecast)

Disclaimer: The opinions expressed in this article are solely those of the featured analyst. The content is intended to be used for informational purposes only. It is very important to do your own analysis before making any investment.
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Rebounds on Upbeat Jobs Data amid Global Tariff Update
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading higher on Wednesday after ADP released its National Employment Report for February.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During the month, private employers added 63,000 jobs, marking the highest growth since July 2025 and topping the consensus estimate of 50,000. However, job growth remains concentrated in certain sectors. The education and health services sector added 58,000 jobs, while the professional and business services sector shed 30,000 jobs.

"But with hiring concentrated in only a few sectors, our data shows no widespread pay benefit from changing jobs," said ADP Chief Economist Dr. Nela Richardson. "In fact, the pay premium for switching employers hit a record low in February."

Meanwhile, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told CNBC that President Trump would raise the global tariff rate to 15% from 10% "likely sometime this week." After the Supreme Court declared Trump's tariffs under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) illegal, he turned to another legal route, Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974.

The statute allows a maximum tariff rate of 15% for up to 150 days to address a major balance-of-payments deficit. Bessent added that he expects tariff rates to revert to their prior rates before the ruling within five months as the administration conducts Section 301 and 232 investigations.

Before the market open, reports circulated that Iran had reached out to the U.S. to engage in peace talks one day after being attacked. However, Iran denied the claims and characterized them as "pure falsehood and psychological warfare."

The Dow Jones is up by 0.58% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Which Defense ETF Is Most Exposed to the U.S. and Iran Conflict?
data.description:
Rising tensions in the Middle East have pushed defense stocks higher, which has also drawn more attention to defense-focused exchange-traded funds. In particular, three defense ETFs appear to be especially well-positioned to benefit from the escalation involving Iran:

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

  • iShares U.S. Aerospace & Defense ETF (ITA)
  • Global X Defense Tech ETF (SHLD)
  • Invesco Aerospace & Defense ETF (PPA)

However, ITA stands out because it has the largest exposure to major U.S. defense contractors. In fact, the fund is heavily concentrated in companies such as RTX (RTX) and Lockheed Martin (LMT), which produce weapons systems that are in high demand during military operations.

For instance, RTX manufactures Patriot missile systems and Tomahawk missiles, both of which are widely used for regional defense. Meanwhile, Lockheed Martin produces the F-35 fighter jet and THAAD missile interceptors, which are key components of modern military operations.

Different Types of Exposure

At the same time, other defense ETFs provide different types of exposure to the same trend. For example, SHLD focuses more on defense technology and advanced digital systems rather than traditional aerospace hardware. As a result, the fund includes companies like Palantir (PLTR), which analysts believe could benefit from increased demand for AI-powered intelligence, surveillance, and threat detection. Meanwhile, PPA takes a diversified approach by holding 61 companies across the aerospace, defense, and homeland security industries, thereby spreading the risk across the entire defense supply chain.

Wall Street's Take

Turning to Wall Street, of the defense ETFs pictured below, analysts see the most upside potential in the ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF (ARKX). In fact, ARKX's price target of $38.75 per share implies 20.6% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Walmart Stock (WMT) Wilts as PhonePe Faces $5B IPO Valuation Slump
data.description:
Walmart (WMT)-backed fintech firm PhonePe is set to be targeting a valuation of between $9 billion to $10.5 billion for its Indian IPO.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Valuation Dials Down

That is down from hopes last year that it would reach a valuation of around $15 billion. It is also a step down from the $12 billion valuation the payments platform achieved during a 2023 fundraising round.

According to PhonePe's IPO filing, Walmart plans to trim its stake in the company, while Microsoft (MSFT) and Tiger Global are set to exit their investments. Walmart currently holds a 72% stake in the business.

In all, the three companies will sell 50.7 million shares in the PhonePe IPO.

PhonePe, which competes with Google (GOOGL) Pay and Paytm in India, filed for its IPO in September 2025 and aims to complete the process by April 2026. The timeline could shift depending on capital market conditions, including any impact from the ongoing Iran war. PhonePe's listing would make it India's second-largest fintech IPO, after Paytm's $20 billion debut in 2021.

PhonePe has more than 610 million registered users and handles 340 million rupees worth of transactions per day.

India Loves Digital Wallets

PhonePe was founded in 2015 and is now the largest payments platform in India. According to research by PYMNTS Intelligence, digital wallets are becoming increasingly popular in India – the world's most populous country with 1.4 billion people.

Digital wallets are the preferred payment method for 55% of retail purchases in the country, boosted by a more affluent population, a growing middle class, and the embracing of technology. The growth has also been helped by the Indian government's push for digital transactions.

PhonePe had been part of Flipkart, an Indian-based e-commerce group that is also majority-owned by Walmart.

Is WMT a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On TipRanks, WMT has a Strong Buy consensus based on 27 Buy and 2 Hold ratings. Its highest price target is $150. WMT stock's consensus price target is $138.43, implying an 8.36% upside.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Don't Be Fooled by the Rally! Polymarket Odds That Bitcoin (BTC) Will Fall Below $50,000 Jump to 60%
data.description:
While retail investors might be celebrating that Bitcoin (BTC) is again trading above $70,000, traders and analysts aren't convinced and continue to bet against the largest cryptocurrency.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In fact, a growing number of traders on Polymarket are betting that Bitcoin's price will fall below the key threshold of $50,000 in coming months. According to the predictions market, the number of professional traders who think that BTC will sink below $50,000 has risen to 60%.

And it could get worse from there. Several analysts warn that Bitcoin falling to $50,000 could precipitate a sharp downward spiral for the cryptocurrency. Polymarket contracts currently show a 39% chance that Bitcoin will fall as low as $40,000 before the current carnage is over.

Is Bitcoin a Safe Haven for Investors?

On social media platforms and chat boards, investors are cheering as Bitcoin's price rises back to $73,000 from as low as $65,000 a day earlier. Many investors are saying that BTC is proving its use case as a safe haven asset in times of market turmoil and geopolitical upheaval.

But again, traders, analysts, and institutional investors disagree. Several analysts say BTC's price is rising quickly as options traders who bet on the price falling are being forced to cover their short bets by purchasing Bitcoin.

In a note to clients, analysts at JPMorgan Chase ($JPM ) say the short-term outlook for Bitcoin and other crypto remains bearish as hopes fade for Federal Reserve interest rate cuts this year and amid continued outflows from exchange-traded funds (ETFs) that track the spot price of BTC and other digital assets..

Bitcoin's Three-Month Performance

The chart below shows that Bitcoin has struggled in recent months and declined nearly 25% in the last 12 weeks as the selloff in cryptocurrencies that began in October 2025 has continued.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) GTC 2026 Is Coming: Here's What to Expect from Huang's Big AI Event
data.description:
Nvidia's (NVDA) GTC 2026 is set to begin soon and is one of the biggest tech events of the year. The four-day conference, running from March 16 to March 19 in San Jose, will focus on the future of AI and accelerated computing. NVDA CEO Jensen Huang's keynote on March 16 is expected to be the highlight, with investors and developers watching closely for new updates on Nvidia's AI chips, systems, and software.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Jensen Huang's Keynote Takes Center Stage

Huang will deliver the keynote from the SAP Center on March 16 at 11 a.m. PT. The presentation is expected to outline Nvidia's latest progress across the full AI stack — from accelerated computing and AI factories to open models, agentic systems, and physical AI.

Here are some of the key highlights expected at GTC 2026:

  1. Nvidia's "Five Layers" of AI: A major theme this year is Nvidia's view that AI is built across five layers: energy, chips, infrastructure, models, and applications. The company plans to show how progress across these areas is helping expand AI systems across industries.
  2. More Than 1,000 Sessions across the AI Ecosystem: The conference will feature more than 1,000 sessions covering areas such as AI data centers, robotics, digital twins, large-scale inference, scientific computing, and enterprise AI. Huang will also host a discussion on the future of open AI models with leaders from several AI organizations.
  3. Focus on Developers and AI Startups: GTC will also highlight the developer ecosystem. The event will include hands-on labs and technical workshops focused on AI and data science. More than 240 startups from Nvidia's Inception program are also expected to showcase technologies in areas such as robotics and generative AI.
  4. AI Research and Real-World Applications: Researchers will present over 150 projects focused on AI models, robotics, and system design. Sessions will also explore how companies are building and scaling AI systems across cloud and edge environments.

Why This Event Matters for NVIDIA

Nvidia's stock is down about 3.4% so far this year, giving the shares a slower start in 2026. Events like GTC often attract strong attention because Nvidia uses the conference to share its latest plans in AI chips and computing.

This year's keynote could bring updates on new AI chips, systems, and other tools used in data centers and AI development. Investors will be watching closely for any announcements that signal stronger demand ahead. Jefferies recently said Nvidia remains one of the most certain AI winners and could still be poised for a breakout.

Is NVDA a Strong Buy?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Nvidia stock based on 38 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations. The average NVDA stock price target of $271.11 indicates 50.57% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: CoreWeave Stock (CRWV) Jumps after New Partnership with Perplexity
data.description:
Artificial intelligence search company Perplexity has entered into a partnership with cloud computing firm CoreWeave (CRWV) to use its data centers for new AI services. As a result, shares of CoreWeave surged at the time of writing. According to CoreWeave CEO Mike Intrator, the agreement is an important step toward diversifying the company's customer base. In an interview with Axios, Intrator explained that the deal shows how more AI companies are choosing CoreWeave's platform.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Importantly, he noted that customers are not just looking for raw computing power, but rather a complete AI cloud platform that's designed specifically for AI workloads. Similarly, Max Hjelm, CoreWeave's senior vice president of revenue, emphasized that AI applications in real-world environments require much more than infrastructure. Instead, they need to be reliable and include end-to-end tools built for AI systems.

Meanwhile, Perplexity believes that the partnership will help it expand its infrastructure and scale its AI services more effectively. Chief business officer Dmitry Shevelenko said that the company was impressed by CoreWeave's technical expertise and its partner-focused approach that helps AI-native businesses grow faster. As part of the agreement, Perplexity will use Nvidia (NVDA) GB200 NVL72-powered clusters to run AI inference workloads. At the same time, CoreWeave will deploy Perplexity Enterprise Max across its organization so employees can search both the web and internal documents more efficiently.

Is CRWV Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on CRWV stock based on 11 Buys, eight Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average CRWV price target of $118.29 per share implies 48.5% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Apple (AAPL) Launches 'Most Affordable Laptop Ever' in Challenge to Windows PC Makers
data.description:
Apple's (AAPL) latest target with its refreshed lineup is the makers of Chromebooks and Windows personal computers. On Wednesday, the consumer electronics giant launched the MacBook Neo, a $599 13-inch PC, dubbing it its "most affordable laptop ever."

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The launch comes as soaring memory-chip prices squeeze PC makers' profit margins, and the market is bracing for its steepest drop in device shipments in over a decade. The MacBook Neo puts Apple in direct competition with Chromebook makers using Alphabet's (GOOGL) ChromeOS and producers of Windows PCs powered by Microsoft's (MSFT) operating system.

The largest such vendors include Lenovo (LNVGY), HP (HPQ), and Dell Technologies (DELL), with other significant players such as Acer, ASUS (ASUUY), and Samsung (SSNLF).
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Oil Could Skyrocket to $100, Warns Goldman Sachs
data.description:
The price for a barrel of Brent crude oil (BZ) has risen by 10% over the past week, although Goldman Sachs warns that extended disruptions to the Strait of Hormuz could send prices even higher.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The strait handles 20% of global oil flows and has been operating at roughly 15% capacity since the U.S. and Israel launched strikes on Iran over the weekend. Goldman's worst-case scenario is for the passage to continue operating at a low volume for five weeks, which could send prices above $100. Another factor that may increase prices is damage to oil infrastructure.

Oil Prices Could Spike Amid Strait of Hormuz Disruptions

Goldman's base case is for the strait to operate at 15% capacity for five more days, followed by a month-long recovery to normal volumes. Earlier this week, the firm raised its second-quarter Brent crude oil price target by $10 to $76. The firm also lifted its fourth-quarter estimate to $66.

Earlier this week, JPMorgan warned that oil prices could surge to $120 in the event of a prolonged U.S.-Iran conflict, adding that Gulf producers could only maintain normal production for 25 days in the event that the Strait of Hormuz is completely blocked.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'Significant Step,' Says Top Analyst on SoFi's New Deal
data.description:
SoFi Technologies (SOFI) is drawing fresh Wall Street attention after announcing that its SoFiUSD stablecoin will be used as a settlement option across the Mastercard (MA) network. Top Keefe Bruyette analyst Tim Switzer described the partnership as a "significant development" for the company.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Switzer said SoFiUSD now joins other major stablecoins that already have ties with Mastercard. That move strengthens SoFi's position as one of the first U.S. banks to push a regulated, bank-issued stablecoin into a global card network.

The analyst said the consumer impact is still unclear. Even so, he believes SoFi "has the chance to become a significant player" as the stablecoin space evolves.

Early Move Could Matter in a Crowded Field

Switzer said the stablecoin market may not remain fragmented for long. As use grows, it could narrow to a few large players with wide reach and strong network ties. By partnering with Mastercard early, SoFi may gain an edge as the market takes shape.

At the same time, he kept an Underperform rating on the shares with a $20 price target, signaling that while the strategic move is a big step, valuation concerns remain.

The partnership places SoFiUSD inside one of the world's largest payment networks and marks a new step in the push to connect regulated digital dollars with everyday card transactions.

What Is SoFi's Price Target?  

According to TipRanks' consensus, SOFI stock has a Hold consensus rating based on five Buys, seven Holds, and three Sells assigned in the last three months. At $25.96, the average SoFi stock price target implies a 39.49% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
